steps.title: Use the sign up pattern
steps.content: The sentence says students can "sign up" followed by the purpose of signing up. Standard English uses the infinitive construction "sign up to" plus a verb to express that purpose.

steps.title: Reject wrong verb forms
steps.content:
• Choice B: "Learned" is a past-tense or participial form and cannot express the purpose after "sign up."
  • Choice C: "Are learning" creates an ungrammatical construction after "sign up."
  • Choice D: The bare verb "learn" is missing the required "to."
The answer is A.
